sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) i.q. עִיר a city, which see (hence pl. עָרִים). In sing. עָר Num 21:15 Deu 2:9, and fully עָר־מֹואָב (city of Moab), Num 21:28 Isa 15:1, pr.n. of the metropolis of Moab, situated on the southern shore of Arnon, Gr. Ἀρεόπολις (which those who did not know the true origin, rendered city of Mars); Abulfeda ماب and الربة, now called Rabba. See Relandi Palæstina, p. 577; Burckhardt’s Reise nach Syrien, p. 640.

(2) an enemy, see the root עִיר No. 2 , 1Sa 28:16. Plur. Psa 9:7 139:20 .

ער

Ch. i.q. Heb. No. 2, Dan 4:16.
